titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Donald Nicholson-Smith Papers
titlestmt.author: by Michael Rush and Arthur Fournier

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday December 5, 2024 at 23:29

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 98 (computer files): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Arthur Fournier Fine & Rare LLC on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2022.

bioghist.head: Donald Nicholson-Smith
bioghist.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith is a literary translator and writer. Born in England but a longtime resident of New York City, Nicholson-Smith has translated a wide range of non-fiction and fiction. Areas of particular importance are the writings of the Situationist International, psychology, psychoanalysis, social criticism, history, and literature. Nicholson-Smith was a member of the English section of the Situationist International between 1965 and 1967. After being expelled from the Situationist International in 1967, Nicholson-Smith co-founded the radical collective King Mob, which was active in London during the late 1960s and early 1970s. The French government named Nicholson-Smith Chevalier des Arts et Lettres in recognition of his services to French literature in translation.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Donald Nicholson-Smith papers consist of correspondence, writings, and translations documenting his work as a writer and translator of many works including the writings of the Situationist International. It also includes records of the English radical collective King Mob.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Donald Nicholson-Smith Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. Correspondence contains incoming and outgoing letters including third-party correspondence documenting Nicholson Smith's involvement with Situationist International (SI) in Paris and London, as well as his work as co-publisher of King Mob Echo, and professional work as a translator. Printed emails, incoming letters and carbon copies of outgoing correspondence constitute the bulk of the materials in this group of items. Other materials include clippings, printed email attachments and articles, as well as other related texts and images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NYC colleague. Translator of Victor Serge and others. Author of book on May '68.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Historian and theorist of anarchism and the SI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend of Donald Nicholson-Smith. Collaborator on screenplay: "Bonfire Night."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comrade of Donald Nicholson-Smith. Anthropologist. Publisher (Egregore Edns.) of T.J. Clark and Donald Nicholson-Smith October article (French translation).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith correspondence concerning Graeber/Piketty article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close friend of Donald Nicholson-Smith. Close friend of Bruce Elwell. Career in film industry (continuity).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member, EdN. Translator of Dwight MacDonald et al. Author on architecture etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close friend from Cambridge days. Founder, Charter 88 and OpenDemocracy (online newspaper). Partner: Judith Herrin, historian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acquaintance. Student of SI and Debord. Friend of Maria Rosa Lehman (included here.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exchange with Situationists, concerning the dialectics of liberation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Participant in "Strasbourg scandal" of 1966. Single original letter to Guy Debord + mimeographed article re: Rudi Dutschke

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member, Echanges et mouvement (Paris)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor, Drunken Boat. Collector of SI materials etc. Friend.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Correspondence with Donald Nicholson-Smith et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parisian society doctor, physician to Debord, et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Supporter at French Cultural Services (NY); now a univ. professor at Nanterre (literature).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Novelist. Editor, Brooklyn Rail.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member, King Mob. Correspondence with Donald Nicholson-Smith from 1990s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Writer. Poet. Member, Retort group (Bay Area)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close friend. Translator of Manchette and editor at City Lights.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes important letter by Louise Crowley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Founder, Processed World; Critical Mass. Writer, historian of SF, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member, King Mob circle. Leeds U. Moved to Australia. Wrote about Baudrillard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor, Le Bathyscaphe etc. (Montreal)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daughter of Robert Chasse

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert and Evelyn and Valerie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American writer and Situationist, was a member of the Council for the Liberation of Daily Life, which was founded in New York City in the fall of 1967, and then of the American Section of the Situationist International from 1969 to his exclusion in 1970. Marterials by Evelyn Chasse (wife) and Valerie Chasse (daughter) are included in the file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bookstore and asylum for refugee artists and creators

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishers and booksellers. Donald Nicholson-Smith's translation of Mension, THE TRIBE

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Art historian. Friend since university. Situationist. Collaborator on Ten Days; October article: "Why Art Can't Kill..."; Debord's Memoires.etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend from schooldays. Archaeologist. King Mob member. Travelled to Algeria with Donald Nicholson-Smith and Cathy Pozzo di Borgo (1964).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend since university days. King Mob's East End branch. Urban anthropologist. Notorious "Dr John" of 144 Piccadilly occupation. Donald Nicholson-Smith has edited many writings of his.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anarchist work resister; copious editor and publisher, including Donald Nicholson-Smith and TJC's October article in French edition (Egregores, Marseilles.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishers of Donald Nicholson-Smith translated work on Chinese medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "D"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Partner of Guy Debord. Linguistic studies (Roma slang). Poet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Founder of The Situationist International

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journalist and collaborator with René Viénet and assistant director of the film, Chinois, encore un effort pour être révolutionnaires.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "E"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American writer, publisher, and member of the American Section of the Situationist International from 1969 to his exclusion in 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ex-wife of Bruce Elwell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor of Book Wooks (London)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "F" including an important latter by Dominique Frager (member of the Socialisme ou Barbarie circle.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Director, EDMA. Stepfather of Cathy Pozzo di Borgo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "G" including an important latter by Dominique Frager (member of the Socialisme ou Barbarie circle.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Old friend. Writer. Classicist. Surrealist Group of Paris. Translator into French (notably of Agamben).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writer on Melville, Korean working class, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writer on Guy Debord.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist. Leading light, King Mob.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Follower and translator of Victor Serge

did.unittitle: Groupe anarchiste de Menilmontant, International anarchiste.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Groupe anarchiste de Menilmontant, International anarchiste, correspondents with the SI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "H"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translator, writer on Surrealist painting and cinema.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcendental Students movement member, and later participant in the national Bioneers conference (www.bioneers.org) and the Eco-Metropolis conference (www.ecometropolis.org) in New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Son and literary executor of J.-P. Manchette. (See also extensive correspondence with Headline in the Manchette translation files.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Taiwan couturiere. Bookseller. Partner of F. Zylberberg (Additional correspondence with Hong in Vienet files.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anarchist publisher, London. Reprints of King Mob, Heatwave, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor (Verso); translator; literary agent. Friend.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anarchist group in correspondece with the SI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Internal and external correspondence of the SI, accumulated through Nicholson-Smith's role as English language translator and secretary to the group, circa 1966-1967

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Debord biographer, translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith. Prolific political writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Belfast academic. Crime fiction blogger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professor of political science at Paris 8-Saint-Denis University and member of the Paris Center for Sociological and Political Research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "K"

did.unittitle: Karam, Anwar and Leder, Sara and family*

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friends. Ex-Echanges et Mouvement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist. Chief author of Strasbourg pamphlet. Archive at Beinecke.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Post-Situationist translator and publisher. Archive at Beinecke.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "L"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Novelist and literary theorist. Translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith. (See also extensive correspondence with Lang in the Cruel Tales from the 13th Floor translation files.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Archivist of Letterists and Situationists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: English follower of Vaneigem. Publisher of small magazine, Spectacular Times (issues included in file.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film distributor and record producer (SI songs etc.). Parisian anarchist close to SI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "M"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Radical filmmaker. Member of Newsreel. Codirected with Cathy Pozzo di Borgo, his spouse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith friend, playwright, film director based in Montreal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cultural historian. Popular music, Punk, Situationists etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist. One letter to Vienet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Novelist and co-author of Retort group's AFFLICTED POWERS.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: English translator of Situationist works, Alice Debord, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parisian radical and small publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Long-term friend. Member, King Mob. Book on Portuguese Revolution of 1974; other writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Old friend. Historian of the "outer left". "Fag Hag," a memoir, trans. Donald Nicholson-Smith (2022)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leading light of BLACK MASK and UAWMF

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German historian of the SI, author of PHANTOM AVANTGARDE etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Academic, scholar of Manchette

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spanish anarchist group and publisher

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member of Black & Red, widow of Fredy Perlman, translator of Debord

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to SI (Debord, Khayati, Donald Nicholson-Smith). Cultural critic. Italian academic (Rome). Writings on Debord et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Historian of the SI, etc: VISIONS OF THE CITY (2005)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mother of Raoul Vaneigem's sons Ariel and Tristan.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 98: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Radical publisher whose lists including numerous works translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith, including several titles by Vaneigem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. King Mob member. IT writer and editor. Blogger on politics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One letter to the SI. Limoges.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anarchist and poet. Also a reporter from London, Paris and the US for Italian newspapers in the 1960s and 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist. Editor, HEATWAVE; REBEL WORKER #6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Emails concerning translations of 10 Day that Shook the University into French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bay Area discussion group. Zoom-based during Covid. Book: AFFLICTED POWERS (2005), on Iraq war, etc. See also Boal, Iain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chicago Surrealist. 1 letter to Debord.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various items of correspondence filed under "S"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Novelist. Intro to Manchette, THE MAD AND THE BAD.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Translator of Debord and Vienet films.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend. Moving spirit of Archipelago Books, Donald Nicholson-Smith's publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publisher of three Donald Nicholson-Smith translations. Naveen Kishore, owner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishers of Manchette and Jonquet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early Vaneigem translator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translator, science writer, Donald Nicholson-Smith collaborator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thinker, Theorie communiste etc. Boule champion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anarchist , Etcetera (group and periodical), Barcelona.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Important letters concerning the Strasbourg affair and its fallout, with correspondence by Mustapha Khayati, Edith and Theo Frey, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Student-movement activist, author, and comrade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member of Êchanges et Mouvement, the Francophone network of council communist inspired militants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paris small publisher close to Vaneigem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Irish correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A sociologist and a Directeur d'études at the Ecole des Hautes Etudes en Sciences Sociales (EHESS, Paris).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publisher, Les Prairies Ordinaires.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist. Friend. Translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Situationist ca. 1970. Published SI texts in USA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishers of Donald Nicholson-Smith translation of Baudrillard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend and collaborator. Situationist, filmmaker, publisher, anti-Maoist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close friend. Art historian. Partner to Tim Clark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translator and teacher at Yale. Close friend. Collaborator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondent in Berkeley to and from SI members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Filmmaker and chronicler of King Mob

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close friend of Donald Nicholson-Smith. Early critic of the SI in USA. Professor of comparative literature.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prime movers of King Mob.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berkeley comrades of Donald Nicholson-Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Friend and collaboraotor. Intimate of Debord and IS. Lifelong companion of Vienet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. King Mob Echo records contains manuscripts and printed ephemera realted to the London circle of King Mob's actions in the late 1960s.

did.unittitle: King Mob manuscripts and typescripts, many with notes by Donald Nicholson-Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles for the serial

did.unittitle: Totality for Kids. Early translation of Vaneigem by Christopher Gray [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation of pamphlet text

did.unittitle: Drafts in typescript for an unpublished English-language Situationist International journal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript drafts with extensive annotations

did.unittitle: China: The Critical Mass of Ideology [translation of SI text by Clark, T.J.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation of pamphlet text

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed issues of the serial

did.unittitle: Clipped images for use in King Mob as illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Source material file for King Mob

did.unittitle: Various pamphlets and handbills issued by members of the King Mob Circle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed items of ephemera. Includes: Circuit Magazine exchange, "Captive Words: Preface to a Situationist Dictionary" booklet, "Unitary Urbanism" booklet, Thesis on the Commune, Occupation of L.S.E., The white paper on industrial sabotage, Manifesto of Rationalism , Agro Pilot Issue, Bash Street School Magazine No. 1, Commune of the Streets, "Art Schools Are Dead In It's Advance The Fire Shall Cease And Judge Everything", "Student Revolt is Dead" booklet, "The Death of Art Spells the Murder of Artist", "Monsieur Krivine N'a Rien Appris Ni Rien Oublie" broadside, Powis Square Gardens (occupation), and "'It Was Meant to be Great But It's Horrible', Confessions: S. Claus 1968".

did.unittitle: Original art and photomechanicals for English-language version of "In our spectacular society where all you can see is things and their price…"
Why Art Can't Kill the Situationist International T. J. Clark; Donald Nicholson-Smith October, Vol. 79, Guy Debord and the Internationale Situationniste. (Winter, 1997)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, proofs, photographs, and notes for the much-cited article by Donald Nicholson-Smith and Clark. No manuscript is present, but the file contains useful exchanges with editors at October and photographs that were used in the printed issue.

did.unittitle: Various children's unpublished book proposals, notes, and drafts
Topsy and Tim: First French Words

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished life narrative oral history transcript retained by Donald Nicholson-Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes for a book or article, unpublished.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional writing on behalf of various publishers.

did.unittitle: Untitled notes concening 1965 vsit to Algeria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Field notes by Donald Nicholson-Smith and Ian Clegg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screenplay for a fictional feature length film, written by Donald Nicholson-Smith and collaborator David Attoe. Contains short stories and additional writings of David Attoe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of articles produced by Donald Nicholson-Smith for Colin Ward's serial, Anarchy.

did.unittitle: Internal Documents of the Situationist International
Dauvé, Gilles (Jean Barrot). Critique of the S.I. (English. Translator unknown)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: text

did.unittitle: Riesel, Rene. English translations of I.S. no. 12 (Translator unknown.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: texts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: bibliographies

did.unittitle: Various. Manuscript notes on publications by the Situationist International
LISTE ETABLIE EN AVRIL 67 annulant les précédentes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fourteen (14) leaves document providing various lists of IS members and sympathizers in Paris and elsewhere. Includes names of bookstores, publishers, and labor organizations. Front cover marked in ink, "R. Desbris."

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Pour un jugement revolutionnaire de l'art [1962?]
Diangani, Lungela. 1. La revolution dans les pays de l'Afrique noire. [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph. The document is labeled "Ndiangani" in black ink at lower right corner of p. 4.

did.unittitle: Frey, Theo. Contribution au debat theorique sur le moment revolutionnaire. [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph. The document is labeled "Theo" in black ink at upper right corner of p. 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph. The document is labeled "Edith" in black ink at upper right corner of p. 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schneider, Frey [Edith and Theo], Garnault, Holl, etc.

did.unittitle: Harstein, Anton. Rapport a la 8' conférence de l'IS : 1. Pour un art d'agitation revolutionnaire. Aspects practiques ; 2. La situation dans les pays de l'Est. (Roumanie, Hongrie.) [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph. The document is labeled "Anton" in black ink at upper right corner of p. 1.

did.unittitle: Haftman, Werner. Gutachtliche Äußerung über das Heft 6 der Gruppe Spur. [196?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine (9) leaves (typewritten text reproduced in mimeograph copy.) Described by Donald Nicholson-Smith as an "orthodox" IS text, likely produced by a stundent in the Strasbourg circle.

did.unittitle: Garneault, Jean. Pour une definition precise de l'IS en tant qu'organisation. [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph. The document is labeled "Jean" in black ink at upper right corner of p. 1.

did.unittitle: Unknown author. Manuscript / typescript notes on origins of S.I.
Various. Documents pour une discussion sur l'orientation de l'I.S. (septembre 1963)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nineteen (19) leaves collation of texts in French (typewritten in carbon copy with manuscript additions, recto only, signed in type "GUY") dated "septembre 1963." Texts concerning Debord's theoretical interchange with Attila Kotànyi, and the relationship between poetry, art and revolutionary politics, marked in purpled in "1" on upper lefthand corner.

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Rapport a la VII' Conference de l'IS : sur le caractère principal de notre activité commune. [1966?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 pp. text printed recto only, mimeograph with hand corrections and additions. The document is labeled "Guy" in black ink at upper right corner of p. 1.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. Sur la réalisation de l'art et la fin de la culture. [196?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three (3) mimeographed leaves, joined at the upper left corner with a metal staple. IS text by Vaneigem in ten numbered sections concerning the creativity, art, and the end of culture.

did.unittitle: Vienet, Rene. English translation of Enragés et Situationists [196?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith is not the translator.

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. A Short History of the Same Place. [drafts, 198?-2000s]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fiction ; two drafts. Unpublished.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nonfiction book on architecture and urbanism ; two drafts. Unpublished.

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. The Art of It [various drafts]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various fiction and non-fiction works by Chasse. Unpublished. [from original listing]

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. Notes in the Margins [various drafts]
Chasse, Robert. The Natural History of a Bad Attitude [various drafts]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Donald Nicholson-Smith notes and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. Land of Giants [various drafts, 1960s-2000s.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished work by Chasse.

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. An Ordinary Murder [various drafts]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished work by Chasse.

did.unittitle: Chasse, Robert. Purple Ravens/World at War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished work by Chasse.

did.unittitle: Cohen, Phil. Working papers and offprints. [circa 1960s-2000s]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various booklets, papers, and books chapters. Includes "On the Youth Question", "New Ethnicities" and other papers, including Cohen's work on strategies for cultivating anti-racism.

did.unittitle: Cohen, Phil. Untitled manuscript on race ; Studies in Learning Regeneration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various unpublished works and Palgrave MacMillian business file.

did.unittitle: Cohen, Phil. Archive that, Comrade! : left legacies and the counter culture of remembrance ; Graphologies [2000s]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Study of archives and left political history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of essays, mostly unpublished

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Texts for Cohen's website with developmental notes and edits by Donald Nicholson-Smith

did.unittitle: Cohen, Phil. Unhoming of Familiar Worlds ; Reading Room Only (A memoir)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memoir by Cohen, and and unpublished collection of essays

accessrestrict.p: Box 98: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Cohen, Phil. Writings on London Olympics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cultural history and critique of the 2012 Olympics

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 98: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets, booklets, and texts linked to Situationist International members Guy Debord, Raoul Vaneigem, Mustapha Khayati, and Donald Nicholson-Smith ; includes process files for Donald Nicholson-Smith contributions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various issues of Bracken's pro-Situ publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets, handbills, and serials by the member of Situationist International

did.unittitle: Elwell, Ruth. Lower East Side documentary film project notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes towards an unfinished film project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished autobiography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screenplays for fictional and documentary films. Includes promotional material for Robert K. Machover and Catherine Pozzo di Borgo film "Collection and Disposal: a Job for the Birds".

did.unittitle: Salsitz, Norman & Myra (as told to Robert Chasse & Donald Nicholson-Smith). Two Jews in Poland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nonfiction book based on oral history project. Unpublished.

did.unittitle: Salsitz, Norman. Poland - Polish Jews Between the Wars (translation by Donald Nicholson-Smith)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished manuscript drafts and revisions.

did.unittitle: Simmons, John Galbraith. Always Too Nice to the Ladies (screenplay)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screenplay for a fictional feature length film.

did.unittitle: Simmons, John Galbraith. Aline & Valcour (Marquis de Sade)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Simmons with comments by Donald Nicholson-Smith

did.unittitle: Unknown author. Memoirs of a Motherfucker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Narrative concerning participation in the eponymous radical movement.

did.unittitle: Unknown author. Messy's Thanksgiving Day Parade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished détournement.

did.unittitle: Winestine, Zack. The Politics of Passion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Critical history of Situationist International, with input and comments by Donald Nicholson-Smith

did.unittitle: Wise, Dave & Stuart. Various limitted circulation underground publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets, handbills, and serials by the members of King Mob

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with various members of the EdN circle including René Riesel (ex-Situationist International) ; Jaime Semprun ; Olivier Barancy, George Orwell, etc., files include fair copy MSS for Donald Nicholson-Smith translation of Catastrophisme ; other articles in draft.

did.unittitle: L'Encyclopedie des Nuisances / Rene Riesel files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with René Riesel (ex-Situationist International) ; including fair copy Du Progress and clippings of Biotech articles, with correspondence and notes between Donald Nicholson-Smith and Riesel.

did.unittitle: Translations of works related to the Situationist International
Debord, Guy. Society of the Spectacle (book)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translations of Society of the Spectacle by Donald Nicholson-Smith with hand corrections and annotations. Files includes other early translations, some unattributed, perhaps by Fredy Perlman.

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy and Asger Jorn ; Boris Donné. Memoires.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files related to proposed English language edition of Memoires, translated by T.J. Clarke and Donald Nicholson-Smith, with mock-ups, drafts, correspondence with Alice Becker-Ho, various publishers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Debord essays translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith, including an extensively annotated revision of his earlier 1965 translation of "The Decline and Fall of the 'Spectacular' Commodity Economy"

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Revolution and Counter-Revolution in Modern Culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation of pamphlet text.

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy and Alice Becker-Ho. Game of War.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete translation files from MSS to corrected proofs with extensive editorial correspondence.

did.unittitle: Class Wargames Presents: Guy Debord's The Game of War

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 98: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Society of the Spectacle (film - 1978)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original translation of the film text into English by Donald Nicholson-Smith, produced for Lebovici and Debord but unused. File contains extensive correspondence with Debord and Lebovici.

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Society of the Spectacle (film - 2022 - forthcoming?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revised original translation of the film text into English by Donald Nicholson-Smith for the Olivier Assayas produced re-release of Society of the Spectacle (film)

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Réfutation de tous jugements, tant élogieux qu'hostiles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original translation of the film text into English by Donald Nicholson-Smith for the Olivier Assayas produced rerelease of Debord's films

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Film translations business correspondence
Debord, Guy. On the Passage of a Few Persons Through a Rather Brief Unity of Time (film - 2022 - forthcoming?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original translation of the film text into English by Donald Nicholson-Smith for the Olivier Assayas produced rerelease of Debord's films

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Screams In Favour of De Sade (film - 2022 - forthcoming?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original translation of the film text into English by Donald Nicholson-Smith for the Olivier Assayas produced rerelease of Debord's films

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Doctoral thesis sent to Donald Nicholson-Smith for comment

did.unittitle: Debord, Guy. Panegyric (translation by James Brook)

did.unittitle: [Debord, Guy.] Grass, Dominique. Contribution à une Critique de la Société du Spectacle
[Debord, Guy] Conference in Arles on translations of Society of the Spectacle
[Debord, Guy] Conference on Debord at Bibliothèque nationale (BnF)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items collected by Donald Nicholson-Smith for research and translation purposes.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. Revolution of Everyday Life (PM Press 2013 updated translation)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File also contains extensive correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith, Vaneigem and the publisher concerning changes, including a new preface.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. Revolution of Everyday Life (Rebel Press/Left Bank Books, 1983)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File also contains extensive correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith and Vaneigem concerning the effort to translate Revolution of Everyday Life, as well as early efforts to locate an English language publisher, ca. 1974-1990s.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. A Letter to My Children (PM Press 2018)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete translation files from MSS to corrected proofs with extensive editorial correspondenceFile also contains extensive correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith, Vaneigem and editors at PM Press.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. A Declariation of the Rights of Human Beings (Translator: Liz Heron)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Liz Heron with extensive corrections and revisions by Donald Nicholson-Smith. Multiple drafts are present, some with significant annotations.

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul and DNS. Notes for an untitled project being an annotated guide to the beers of Belgium (unpublished)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Project proposal with extensive notes by Vaneigem and Donald Nicholson-Smith on a regional travel guide for those interested in Belgian brewing

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. The Movement of the Free Spirit (Zone, 1998)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith translation of the book by Vaneigem, including extensive editorial correspondence

did.unittitle: Vaneigem, Raoul. A Cavalier History of Surrealism (AK Press, 1999)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donald Nicholson-Smith translation of the book by Vaneigem, including extensive editorial correspondence

did.unittitle: Viénet, René. Peking Duck Soup Donald Nicholson-Smith translations
Viénet, René. Peking Duck Soup reception and reviews

did.unittitle: Viénet, René. René Viénet articles on China
Viénet, René. "Photographs and Notes of a Journey in Southern Formosa" book by John Thomson
Viénet, René. "Leaf in Wind" by Lambert Van der Aalsvoort
Viénet, René. "Instituting a 'Psalmanazar Prize' in Taiwan" by Wei Yannian and translated by Donald-Nicholson Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft samples and entensive editorial correspondence by Donald Nicholson-Smith concerning efforts to translate and publish the Olympe de Gouges biography by Olivier Blanc.

did.unittitle:
Jappe, Anselm. Sic Transit Gloria Artis "The End of Art" for Theordor Adorno and Guy Debord (SubStance
University of Wisconsin Press Issue 90, Volume 28, Number 3, 1999)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple corrected drafts for translation files, mostly proofs. File also contains correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith, Jappe, and SubStance editors.

did.unittitle: Jappe, Anselm. Guy Debord (University of California Press, 1999 ; revised PM Press 2018)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Significant but incomplete translation files, mostly corrected proofs. File also contains extensive correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith, Jappe, and Univ. California Press

did.unittitle: Mension, Jean-Michel. The Tribe. (City Lights Publishers, 2001)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple corrected drafts for translation files, mostly proofs with extensive editorial correspondence. File also contains extensive correspondence between Donald Nicholson-Smith, Mension, and City Lights Books.

did.unittitle: Khayati, Mustapha. 10 Days that Shook the University (translation by Donald Nicholson-Smith and T.J. Clark)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entensive collection of manuscript notes and drafts by T.J. Clark

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corrected drafts, some correspondence with Sanguinetti

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, reviews, articles, correspondence, and contracts related to Donald Nicholson-Smith's translation

did.unittitle: Laplanche, Jean and Pontalis, J.B. The Language of Psychoanalysis

scopecontent.p: Correspondence, articles, and notes related to Donald Nicholson-Smith translation

did.unittitle: Laplanche, Jean and Pontalis, J.B. Files related to Donald Nicholson-Smith translations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, articles, contracts, reviews, related to works of Jean-Bertrand Pontalis and Jean Laplanche translated by Donald Nicholson-Smith. Includes files on The Temptation of Biology: Freud's Theories of Sexuality , Brother of the Above , and The Language of Psychoanalysis .

did.unittitle: Gabel, Dr. Joseph. La Fausse Conscience

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, correspondence, and articles related to the translation of "La Fausse Conscience". Donald Nicholson-Smith was consulted for advice on the translated sample.

did.unittitle: Inhelder, Bärbel. Language and thoughts: Some Remarks on Chomsky and Piaget

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and annotations related to Donald Nicholson-Smith collaboration on translation of "Language and thoughts: Some Remarks on Chomsky and Piaget". Includes July 1978 volume of Journal of Psycholinguistic Research .

did.unittitle: Ortigues, Marie-Cecile and Edmund. African Oedipus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete translation files from MSS to corrected typescripts for the unpublished work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand annotated typescript sent from Voyat to Donald Nicholson-Smith. Includes photocopied dedication from Donald Nicholson-Smith at the beginning of The World of Henri Wallon edited by Gilbert Voyat

did.unittitle: Voyat, Gilbert. The Cognitive Development among Sioux Children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Peraldi, François. The "Great Man" from Vienna to Paris in the Thirties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with publishers. Significant manuscript material is present in the form of corrected drafts, etc.

did.unittitle: Khadra, Jasmina. Cousin K (University of Nebraska Press, 2013)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with publishers. Some manuscript material is present in the form of corrected drafts, etc., most material in electronic form.

did.unittitle: Pey, Serge. The Treasure of the Spanish Civil War (Archipelago, 2020)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with publishers. Significant manuscript material is present in the form of corrected drafts, etc.

did.unittitle: Lang, Luc. Cruel Tales from the Thirteenth Floor (University of Nebraska Press, 2015)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with Luc Lang and publishers. Significant manuscript material is present in the form of corrected drafts, etc.

did.unittitle: Clebert, Jean-Paul and Patrice Molinard. Paris insolite / Paris Vagabond (NYRB, 2016)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with publishers. Near complete manuscript material is present in the form of MSS drafts, anotated typescripts and proofs, fair copies. Also related research files, reception, and events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with publishers. Significant manuscript material is present in the form of annotated typescripts and proofs, fair copies. Also related research files, reception, and events.

did.unittitle: Serge, Victor. A Blaze in the Desert (translated by James Brook) (PM Press 2017)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation notes and correspondence. Comments by DNS on James Brook translation.

did.unittitle: Boni-Claverie, Isabelle and Valérie Tong Cuong. Ou, je suis / Heart of Blackness, etc. (unpublished/unproduced?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence with Boni Claverie and others. Significant manuscript material is present in the form of annotated typescripts and proofs, fair copies.
